name: Quality Tier 4 — GROUP BY + aggregation per dimension
description: GROUP BY single lookup dimension with COUNT/SUM aggregate. Expected = number of distinct groups returned (each row = one group). Tests PromoteGroupByPkToLabel + auto-inference + the FK-walk for grouping on a lookup label rather than the FK column.

Scenarios.Question: ticket count per priority
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT TicketPriorities.Name, COUNT(*) GROUP BY TicketPriorities.Name
Scenarios.Notes: Classic 'X per Y' shape. 4 priorities exist; if every priority has at least one ticket, result is 4 rows.

Scenarios.Question: how many tickets are in each status
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT TicketStatuses.Name, COUNT(*) GROUP BY TicketStatuses.Name
Scenarios.Notes: Only 2 statuses are actually represented (33 Open + 50 Closed/Resolved). Detects whether the LLM groups by the populated value (right) or by all 7 statuses returning some with 0 (wrong).

Scenarios.Question: outages per service type
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT ServiceTypes.NameEn, COUNT(*) GROUP BY ServiceTypes.NameEn
Scenarios.Notes: Different root + different lookup. 4 service types have at least one outage out of 5 total.

Scenarios.Question: total bill amount per status
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT Bills.Status, SUM(Bills.TotalAmount) GROUP BY Bills.Status
Scenarios.Notes: GROUP BY a column on the ROOT table (no join). 3 distinct status values: Paid, Issued, Overdue.

Scenarios.Question: average meter reading per service type
Scenarios.ExpectedShape: SELECT ServiceTypes.NameEn, AVG(MeterReadings.Value) GROUP BY ServiceTypes.NameEn
Scenarios.Notes: AVG-per-dimension. 4 service types represented in meter readings.
